name: Opentrons HTTP API Vocabulary
description: Key terms and concepts used in the Opentrons HTTP API for lab automation robots

terms.definition: A set of instructions defining an automated biology lab workflow, written in Python or JSON format. Protocols are uploaded to the robot and can be analyzed and executed as runs.
An instance of a protocol being executed on a robot. A run tracks state (idle, running, paused, succeeded, failed) and contains the commands, labware, and pipettes loaded during execution.
An atomic robot instruction such as aspirate, dispense, pick up tip, drop tip, move to well, or load labware. Commands are executed sequentially within a run.
A liquid handling instrument attached to the robot's left or right mount. Pipettes aspirate and dispense liquids in specific volumes using interchangeable tips.
A physical container placed on the robot deck, such as a well plate, tip rack, reservoir, or tube rack. Each labware type has a definition specifying its geometry and well positions.
An individual container within a piece of labware identified by name (e.g., 'A1', 'B2'). Wells have geometry including depth, volume, shape, and diameter.
A command to draw liquid into a pipette from a well at a specified volume and flow rate.
A command to expel liquid from a pipette into a well at a specified volume and flow rate.
A command to pick up a pipette tip from a tip rack at a specified well location.
A command to eject a pipette tip into a trash container or tip rack well.

A hardware accessory attached to the robot deck that provides additional functionality, such as a thermocycler, heater-shaker, temperature module, magnetic module, absorbance plate reader, or Flex Stacker.
The Opentrons Flex (OT-3) is Opentrons' advanced lab automation robot with a gripper arm, improved pipetting, larger deck, and support for additional modules. Robot model identifier: 'OT-3 Standard'.
The Opentrons OT-2 is Opentrons' original lab automation robot. Robot model identifier: 'OT-2 Standard'.
The flat work surface of the robot where labware and modules are placed. The OT-2 has a 4x3 numbered slot grid; the Flex has a 4x3 lettered slot grid (A1-D3).
The assignment of fixtures to deck slots on the Opentrons Flex, defining which cutout slots contain trashes, staging areas, or module positions.
A positional correction vector (x, y, z in mm) applied to a specific labware type and location to compensate for deck calibration or labware placement variation.
A dry-run simulation of a protocol that identifies required labware, pipettes, and modules, validates the protocol logic, and reports potential errors without physically moving the robot.
A special run type used for calibration, hardware setup, and tip handling outside of normal protocol execution. Maintenance runs can coexist with protocol runs.
Emergency Stop button on the Opentrons Flex. When engaged, immediately halts all robot motion. The estop status can be queried and acknowledged via the HTTP API.
A variable parameter in a protocol that can be set at run creation time without modifying the protocol file. Supports int, float, boolean, string (choice), and CSV file types.
A Flex-only instrument that can pick up and move labware between deck positions. Attached at the extension mount, separate from pipette mounts.
The 'Opentrons-Version' HTTP request header that must be included on most API calls. Specifies which API version to use (2-4) or '*' for the latest version.
A module that runs PCR protocols by cycling through precise temperature stages. The Flex supports the Thermocycler Module Gen2. Controlled via thermocycler/* commands.
A module that heats and shakes samples simultaneously. Controlled via heaterShaker/* commands for temperature, speed, and labware latch.
A module for performing spectrophotometry measurements on microplates. Controlled via absorbanceReader/* commands.
A Flex-only module that can automatically stack and retrieve labware, enabling automated plate handling workflows.
